What is ESG Assurance?

ESG Assurance is an independent verification process that confirms the accuracy, credibility, and completeness of your sustainability disclosures, whether it’s your Greenhouse Gas (GHG) inventory, Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR), or Annual Sustainability Report (ASR).

Using globally recognized standards like the AA1000 Standard (AA1000AS v3), third-party validates your data and builds stakeholder confidence. It also prepares your organization for regulatory scrutiny, investor evaluation, and global sustainability rankings. At Growlity, we offer independent assurance services tailored to your sustainability reporting needs, delivering credibility, transparency, and audit readiness across your journey.

Scoping & Goal Setting

Define the LCA’s scope, system boundary, and objectives aligned with your product and audience.

System Boundaries

Choose cradle-to-gate, grave, or cradle-to-cradle.

Functional Unit

Set a measurable basis for comparison.

Target Focus

Align with business goals or disclosure needs.

ISO-Compliant Rigor

We follow globally accepted protocols to ensure your LCA stands up to scrutiny.

  • Based on ISO 14040 & 14044 guidelines
  • Transparent, reproducible, and verifiable approach
  • Ready for audits and third-party reviews
  • Meets EPD and industry-specific norms

LCA studies built for global credibility and assurance.

FAQs

On The Side of Ambitious

 Action 

We help brands stand out and grow by creating innovative, results-driven strategies that engage customers and drive success.

What is LCA used for?

Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) is a powerful tool used to measure and analyze the environmental impacts of a product, service, or process across its entire life cycle—from raw material extraction to end-of-life disposal. It helps organizations identify environmental trade-offs, improve sustainability performance, and make data-driven decisions in product design, supply chain management, and operations. LCA is also used to support regulatory compliance, eco-labeling, Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs), and green marketing initiatives.

How do ISO 14040 and 14044 relate?

ISO 14040 and ISO 14044 are the international standards that define how Life Cycle Assessments should be conducted. ISO 14040 outlines the overarching principles, framework, and scope of LCA, including goal definition and system boundaries. ISO 14044 provides detailed guidance on methodology, data requirements, impact assessment, interpretation, and reporting. Together, these standards ensure consistency, transparency, and scientific rigor in LCA studies, and are widely used to validate the quality of environmental disclosures.

Which software is best for LCA?

There are several leading software tools used for conducting LCAs, each with its strengths depending on your project scope and budget: openLCA: A free, open-source tool that is ISO-compliant and widely used for academic, SME, and professional assessments. SimaPro: Known for its robust database access and detailed impact analysis, ideal for product design and corporate reporting. GaBi: Offers comprehensive datasets and is well-suited for industrial applications and complex supply chains. Umberto: Focuses on material and energy flow modeling, offering powerful visualization and data interpretation tools. The best choice depends on your technical needs, available data, and the level of support required.

Do I need LCA for CDP/BRSR/EPD?

While not always mandatory, having a formal Life Cycle Assessment significantly strengthens your environmental disclosures. For CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project), LCA helps in accurately reporting product-level Scope 3 emissions and environmental risks. For BRSR (Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting in India), LCA supports claims of circularity, eco-design, and resource efficiency. For EPD (Environmental Product Declarations), LCA is a core requirement for verifying and publishing third-party certified environmental impact statements. Using LCA adds transparency, credibility, and alignment with globally accepted frameworks.

How long does it take to complete an LCA?

The duration of an LCA project can vary depending on the complexity of the product or system being analyzed, the availability of data, and the desired depth of assessment. A basic screening-level LCA may take 2–4 weeks, while a detailed, ISO-compliant study for EPD or third-party disclosure can take 2–3 months. Timelines also depend on the number of life cycle stages covered, the quality of data, and the need for verification. Starting early with proper data organization can greatly streamline the process.

Explore More

You might also Be Interested In…!!!

RJC Certification

The Responsible Jewellery Council (RJC) is a globally recognized organization that sets standards for the jewellery, gemstone, and watch industry. It promotes responsible business practices, including ethics, human rights, social responsibility, and environmental stewardship across the entire supply chain, from mine to retail. RJC certification demonstrates a company’s commitment to integrity, transparency, and sustainability within the jewellery sector. Being RJC certified builds trust among consumers, stakeholders, and business partners, ensuring accountability and long-term value in all aspects of the jewellery industry.

Read More

RJC Certification

Ecovadis Assessment

EcoVadis is a globally recognized sustainability assessment platform that evaluates a company’s performance across four key pillars: Environment, Labor & Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement. Over 150,000 companies worldwide rely on EcoVadis to benchmark, monitor, and improve their ESG ratings across global supply chains, making it one of the most trusted platforms for sustainability performance. As an EcoVadis consultant and approved training partner, Growlity supports organizations in successfully completing their EcoVadis assessment, improving their EcoVadis score, and strengthening their sustainability credibility. Our tailored consulting ensures higher ESG performance, enhanced compliance, and greater stakeholder confidence, positioning your business for long-term, responsible growth.

Read More

Ecovadis Assessment

ESG Reporting

ESG Reporting refers to the structured disclosure of a company’s Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) performance. It enables businesses to demonstrate how they manage risks and opportunities across climate change, resource efficiency, human rights, ethical governance, diversity, and more. In today’s investor-driven, regulation-focused environment, ESG reporting is no longer optional—it’s a strategic imperative. ESG disclosures influence investment decisions, consumer trust, regulatory compliance, and corporate reputation.

Read More

ESG Reporting
